DNS Leak Test

Incorrect network configurations or faulty VPN/proxy software can lead to your device sending DNS requests directly to your ISP's server, potentially enabling ISPs or other third parties to monitor your online activity.

The DNS Leak Test is a tool used to determine which DNS servers your browser is using to resolve domain names. This test attempts to resolve 50 randomly generated domain names, of which 25 are IPv4-only and 25 are IPv6-only.

Your IP Address
IP Address	185.211.32.87
ISP	Datacamp Limited
Location	United States, San Francisco

TLS Client Test

This page displays your web browser's SSL/TLS capabilities, including supported TLS protocols, cipher suites, extensions, and key exchange groups. It highlights any weak or insecure options and generates a TLS fingerprint in JA3/JA4 formats. Additionally, it tests how your browser handles insecure mixed content requests.

findings.title: Third-party benchmark page loaded (yourinfo.ai)
findings.description: HAR and page excerpt captured for competitive benchmark; third parties may observe exit IP and browser metadata.

question_coverage.question_text: What identifiers are assigned to the user, app install, browser session, and device?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Browser/session signals captured via fingerprint and optional YourInfo probe.

question_coverage.question_text: Are there long-lived client identifiers transmitted during auth or app startup?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Services contacted list enumerates URLs used during harness (may include auth-adjacent endpoints).

question_coverage.question_text: Is the browser fingerprinting surface strong enough to re-identify the same user across sessions?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Fingerprint and BrowserLeaks captures present for re-identification risk assessment.

question_coverage.question_text: What third parties are involved during signup?
question_coverage.answer_summary: No competitor web HAR in this run; configure competitor_probe and surface_urls.

question_coverage.question_text: Are analytics or marketing scripts loaded during signup or checkout?
question_coverage.answer_summary: No competitor web HAR in this run; configure competitor_probe and surface_urls.

question_coverage.question_text: Are these surfaces behind a CDN/WAF?
question_coverage.answer_summary: No competitor web HAR in this run; configure competitor_probe and surface_urls.

question_coverage.question_text: Where is the marketing site hosted (DNS/routing level)?
What CDN/WAF is used?
question_coverage.notes: No web or portal probes in run.

question_coverage.question_text: Does the site leak origin details through headers, TLS metadata, redirects, or asset URLs?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Review web probe headers, redirects, and HAR for origin leaks.

question_coverage.question_text: Which DNS resolvers are used while connected?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Resolver tiers observed (local + external).

question_coverage.question_text: Are DNS requests tunneled (consistent with VPN exit)?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Leak flag=False; see notes.
question_coverage.notes: Heuristic: no obvious public resolver IPs parsed from external page

question_coverage.question_text: Is there DNS fallback to ISP/router/public resolvers?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Leak flag=False; see notes.
question_coverage.notes: Heuristic: no obvious public resolver IPs parsed from external page

question_coverage.question_text: Does DNS leak during connect/disconnect/reconnect?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Connect/disconnect DNS not sampled; use --transition-tests when supported.

question_coverage.question_text: Are DoH or DoT endpoints used?
question_coverage.answer_summary: DoH/DoT not isolated from resolver snapshot; inspect raw captures.

question_coverage.question_text: Are resolvers first-party or third-party?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Leak flag=False; see notes.
question_coverage.notes: Heuristic: no obvious public resolver IPs parsed from external page

question_coverage.question_text: Is the real public IPv4 exposed while connected?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Exit IPv4 185.211.32.87; leak flags dns=False webrtc=False ipv6=False.

question_coverage.question_text: Is the real public IPv6 exposed while connected?
question_coverage.answer_summary: No IPv6 exit or IPv6 not returned by endpoints.

question_coverage.question_text: Is the real IP exposed through WebRTC?
question_coverage.answer_summary: WebRTC candidates captured; leak flag=False.
question_coverage.notes: Exit IP appears in candidate set (expected for tunneled public)

question_coverage.question_text: Is the local LAN IP exposed through WebRTC or browser APIs?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Inspect host candidates vs LAN; see webrtc_notes.
question_coverage.notes: Exit IP appears in candidate set (expected for tunneled public)

question_coverage.question_text: Do leak-check sites disagree about observed IP identity?
question_coverage.answer_summary: All 3 echo endpoints agree on IPv4 185.211.32.87.

question_coverage.question_text: Which domains and IPs are contacted after the tunnel is up?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Post-harness service list captured.

question_coverage.question_text: Which control-plane endpoints are used for auth/config/session management?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Auth/control-plane inventory requires internal docs or app instrumentation.

question_coverage.question_text: Which telemetry endpoints are contacted during connection?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Infer from services_contacted and classified endpoints.

question_coverage.question_text: Is the control plane behind a CDN/WAF?
What exit IP is assigned for each region?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Exit IPv4 185.211.32.87 for location us-california-san-jose-87.

question_coverage.question_text: What ASN announces the exit IP?
question_coverage.answer_summary: ASN 212238 — CDNEXT Datacamp Limited

question_coverage.question_text: What organization owns the IP range?
question_coverage.answer_summary: ASN 212238 — CDNEXT Datacamp Limited

question_coverage.question_text: What reverse DNS exists for the exit node?
question_coverage.answer_summary: PTR lookup errors: ptr_v4: The DNS query name does not exist: 87.32.211.185.in-addr.arpa.

question_coverage.question_text: Does the observed geolocation match the advertised location?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Consistent: exit_geo.location_label matches vpn_location_label ('San Jose, California, United States').

question_coverage.question_text: What external JS files are loaded on the site?
What analytics providers are present?
What cookies are set by first-party and third-party scripts?
Does the site attempt browser fingerprinting?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Fingerprint snapshot captured (harness baseline; does not prove the provider site runs fingerprinting—see THIRDWEB / HAR rows for script-level evidence).

question_coverage.question_text: Does WebRTC run on provider pages?
question_coverage.answer_summary: WebRTC exercised by harness on leak-test pages.

question_coverage.question_text: Does the app talk to telemetry vendors?
question_coverage.answer_summary: App telemetry requires traffic capture or binary analysis; not proven by this harness alone.

question_coverage.question_text: Does the app send connection events to telemetry systems?
question_coverage.answer_summary: App telemetry requires traffic capture or binary analysis; not proven by this harness alone.

question_coverage.question_text: On macOS/Windows/Linux, do helper processes bypass the tunnel?
question_coverage.answer_summary: OS snapshot: Darwin 25.4.0; no process-level tunnel bypass test in this run.

question_coverage.question_text: What leaks during initial connection?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Not sampled; optional --transition-tests or manual observation.

question_coverage.question_text: What leaks during reconnect?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Not sampled; optional --transition-tests or manual observation.

question_coverage.question_text: What leaks if the VPN app crashes?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Crash/kill leak tests not run in this harness by default.

question_coverage.question_text: What is the provider likely able to log based on observed traffic?
question_coverage.answer_summary: Infer logging surface from observable endpoints and services_contacted.

question_coverage.question_text: Are there contradictions between observed traffic and no-logs marketing claims?
question_coverage.notes: No policy fetch or failed fetch.

website_exposure_methodology.evidence_tier_note: Desk automation of website-exposure methodology (Phases 1–9). Do not conflate with client resolver / DNS-leak observations (O); see docs/research-questions-and-evidence.md.

classifications.notes: Heuristic tags from HAR hints + host presence only.
